name: Greenland Ice Sheet collapse
near_term_status: Possible at current warming; risk increases with additional warming
warming_context: Listed among systems possible around current warming in recent reassessments; ice-sheet change unfolds over centuries to millennia once committed.
primary_causes: Surface melting; ice-albedo feedback; ocean/atmosphere warming; black carbon and other regional forcing can worsen melt.
effects: Long-term sea-level rise; coastal displacement; changes in freshwater input to the North Atlantic; ecosystem and infrastructure impacts in coastal regions.
interactions: Freshwater input can weaken North Atlantic circulation; albedo loss amplifies warming; sea-level impacts compound storm surge and coastal adaptation pressure.
domino_summary: Ice loss -> darker surface/ocean exposure -> more absorbed heat -> more melting -> sea-level commitment -> coastal displacement and adaptation stress.
app_card_summary: A slow but high-impact domino: once enough ice loss becomes self-sustaining, sea-level rise can be locked in for centuries.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: ice sheets naturally change, so current loss is not human-linked. Media-literacy prompt: compare short-term variability with long-term mass-balance trends and attribution evidence.
suggested_ui: Large ice-card with a crack animation; downstream dominoes: Sea Level, Coastal Cities, Migration, Adaptation Cost.

name: West Antarctic Ice Sheet collapse
near_term_status: Possible at current warming; some observations suggest parts may be close to irreversible retreat
warming_context: Identified as one of the dangerous tipping elements already within reach at current global heating in the 2022 reassessment.
primary_causes: Warming oceans undercut ice shelves; marine ice-sheet instability; grounding-line retreat; loss of buttressing ice shelves.
effects: Long-term multi-meter sea-level contribution; coastal flood risk; displacement of people and infrastructure; adaptation and insurance stress.
interactions: Sea-level rise magnifies storm damage; freshwater/ocean changes can affect circulation; adaptation failures can cascade into economic and social stress.
domino_summary: Ocean warming -> ice shelf thinning -> grounding line retreat -> self-sustaining ice loss -> sea-level rise -> coastal disruption.
app_card_summary: A hidden-underwater domino: warming ocean water can destabilize ice from below before the damage is obvious from the surface.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: Antarctic ice gain cancels losses. Prompt users to distinguish regional snowfall variability from marine ice-sheet instability and net mass loss.
suggested_ui: Underwater cross-section icon; animated warm current erodes an ice shelf; downstream dominoes: Sea Level, Coastal Risk, Economic Shock.

name: East Antarctic subglacial basin ice loss
near_term_status: Lower near-term likelihood than Greenland/West Antarctica, but severe if triggered
warming_context: Becomes possible at higher warming levels in the 2-3°C/current-policy range in some reassessments.
primary_causes: Warm ocean exposure to marine-based basins; ice-shelf thinning; grounding-line retreat; long-term warming.
effects: Very large long-term sea-level potential; coastal reshaping; high adaptation burden.
interactions: Compounds with Greenland/West Antarctic sea-level contributions; strengthens coastal displacement and infrastructure cascades.
domino_summary: Long-term warming -> basin instability -> irreversible retreat -> major sea-level commitment -> coastal system stress.
app_card_summary: A low-probability but massive-impact domino for the app’s 'severity vs likelihood' lesson.
misinformation_angle: Common confusion: low probability means no risk. Media-literacy prompt: separate likelihood, consequence, and uncertainty.
suggested_ui: Locked vault/ice basin symbol; use as a 'high impact, lower immediacy' card with a warning badge.

name: Abrupt permafrost thaw
near_term_status: Possible at current warming according to reassessments
warming_context: Widespread abrupt permafrost thaw is identified among systems possible at current warming and more likely as warming rises.
primary_causes: Arctic amplification; ground warming; ice-rich soil collapse; wildfire; hydrological change.
effects: Carbon dioxide and methane release; landscape collapse; damage to northern infrastructure; disruption to Arctic Indigenous communities and ecosystems.
interactions: Released greenhouse gases amplify warming; infrastructure damage raises social/economic vulnerability; fires can accelerate thaw.
domino_summary: Arctic warming -> frozen soil thaws -> carbon release -> more warming -> more thaw; local ground collapse damages infrastructure.
app_card_summary: A feedback-loop domino: thaw releases carbon that can intensify the warming that caused the thaw.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: permafrost carbon is too small to matter. Prompt: look at feedback strength, timescale, and uncertainty rather than only immediate emissions.
suggested_ui: Cracked ground + methane bubbles; downstream dominoes: Carbon Feedback, Arctic Infrastructure, Ecosystem Shift.

name: Permafrost carbon-rich deposit collapse
near_term_status: Higher-warming risk; important feedback rather than runaway scenario
warming_context: Carbon-rich permafrost deposits such as Yedoma are discussed as potentially adding about 0.2-0.4°C over time in high-warming scenarios.
primary_causes: Deep thaw; thermokarst collapse; microbial decomposition of ancient organic carbon; wildfire and hydrology shifts.
effects: Additional warming from greenhouse-gas release; landscape collapse; ecosystem and infrastructure disruption.
interactions: Amplifies global warming and can worsen other heat-sensitive tipping systems; interacts with Arctic fire and hydrological change.
domino_summary: Deep thaw -> ancient carbon decomposition -> CO₂/CH₄ release -> warming amplification -> more thaw pressure.
app_card_summary: Use this card to teach feedback strength: serious amplification, but not a Hollywood-style runaway trigger.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading extremes: either dismissing it entirely or exaggerating it as instant apocalypse. Prompt: distinguish amplification from runaway feedback.
suggested_ui: Carbon gauge icon; pair with a 'feedback meter' visualization.

name: Warm-water coral reef die-off
scale: Regional impact with global biodiversity importance
near_term_status: Likely or already very close at current warming; widespread mortality becomes very likely around/above 1.5°C
warming_context: Global Tipping Points sources identify warm-water coral reefs as among the closest tipping systems; recent reports describe reefs as crossing/near a thermal tipping point.
primary_causes: Marine heatwaves; ocean warming; acidification; pollution; overfishing; local ecosystem stress.
effects: Loss of reef biodiversity; fisheries decline; reduced coastal protection; tourism and livelihood losses for hundreds of millions of people.
interactions: Biodiversity loss affects fisheries and food security; degraded reefs reduce coastal storm protection; ocean acidification compounds heat stress.
domino_summary: Ocean heat -> bleaching -> coral mortality -> habitat loss -> fisheries decline -> livelihood/coastal-protection impacts.
app_card_summary: The fastest and most visual domino: heat stress can rapidly bleach reefs and cascade into biodiversity, food, and coastal risk.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: reefs always recover after bleaching. Prompt: distinguish isolated bleaching from repeated heat stress and mass mortality.
suggested_ui: Bright coral icon fading to white; downstream dominoes: Biodiversity, Fisheries, Coastal Protection, Livelihoods.

name: Amazon rainforest dieback
near_term_status: Not necessarily likely at low warming from climate alone, but risk can occur sooner with deforestation and fire
warming_context: Identified as a tipping system; Global Tipping Points reports cite evidence of early warning signals and emphasize deforestation as an interacting driver.
primary_causes: Deforestation; warming; drought; fire; reduced rainfall recycling; land-use change.
effects: Biodiversity loss; carbon release; regional warming; rainfall disruption across South America; harm to Indigenous peoples and local livelihoods.
interactions: Forest loss reduces rainfall recycling, increasing drought/fire risk; carbon release adds warming; biodiversity loss reduces ecosystem resilience.
domino_summary: Deforestation + drought -> less rainfall recycling -> more forest stress/fire -> dieback -> carbon release and rainfall disruption.
app_card_summary: A classic domino-effect system: forest loss can weaken the rainfall cycle that the forest itself depends on.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: CO₂ fertilization will simply make forests thrive. Prompt: compare CO₂ fertilization with heat, drought, fire, and land-use stressors.
suggested_ui: Rainforest card with rainfall-loop animation; downstream dominoes: Rainfall, Biodiversity, Carbon, Agriculture.

name: Boreal forest southern dieback
near_term_status: Possible around 1.5°C in reassessments; risk rises with warming, drought, pests, and fire
warming_context: Boreal forest southern dieback becomes possible around Paris-range warming in the 2022 reassessment.
primary_causes: Heat stress; drought; pests; wildfire; soil moisture loss; ecosystem disturbance.
effects: Forest mortality; habitat loss; carbon release; smoke/air-quality impacts; community and forestry-sector disruption.
interactions: Fire releases carbon and darkens surfaces; forest loss changes albedo and habitat; drought/fire feedbacks reinforce dieback.
domino_summary: Heat/drought -> pests/fire -> forest mortality -> carbon release and habitat loss -> further warming/ecosystem stress.
app_card_summary: A forest-stress domino where heat, insects, drought, and fire can reinforce each other.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: warming only expands northern forests, so forests benefit overall. Prompt: distinguish northern expansion from southern dieback and biodiversity loss.
suggested_ui: Evergreen icon splitting into green-to-brown gradient; downstream dominoes: Fire, Carbon, Habitat, Air Quality.

name: Boreal forest northern expansion
near_term_status: Possible around 1.5°C; not simply positive because it disrupts tundra systems and albedo
warming_context: Boreal northern expansion is discussed alongside boreal southern dieback as a possible tipping dynamic.
primary_causes: Warmer growing seasons; shrub and tree encroachment into tundra; changing snow and soil conditions.
effects: Tundra habitat loss; altered albedo; Indigenous livelihood impacts; complex carbon-cycle consequences.
interactions: Darker vegetation can absorb more heat than snow/tundra; interacts with permafrost thaw and wildfire regimes.
domino_summary: Warming -> trees/shrubs move north -> surface darkening -> more heat absorption -> tundra/permafrost stress.
app_card_summary: A 'not all green is good' card: tree expansion can still destabilize cold-region systems.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: more vegetation automatically offsets climate change. Prompt: ask what ecosystem is being replaced and what happens to albedo/permafrost.
suggested_ui: Tree-line advancing over snow; downstream dominoes: Albedo, Tundra Habitat, Permafrost.

name: Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation collapse
near_term_status: Possible around 1.5°C; larger risk at higher warming; observational warning signals are debated/uncertain
warming_context: AMOC is one of four ocean/atmosphere circulation tipping points identified by the Global Tipping Points Report.
primary_causes: Freshwater input from ice melt; ocean warming; salinity changes; weakening density-driven circulation.
effects: Major North Atlantic regional cooling; shifts in rainfall and monsoons; sea-level changes; ecosystem and agriculture disruption.
interactions: Greenland melt can weaken AMOC; AMOC changes can affect monsoons and regional climate; impacts compound food/water stress.
domino_summary: Ice melt/freshwater -> weaker ocean sinking -> circulation slowdown -> rainfall/temperature shifts -> food/water/ecosystem impacts.
app_card_summary: The ocean-conveyor domino: one circulation shift can rearrange regional climate patterns far from the trigger.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: regional cooling would disprove global warming. Prompt: distinguish global average warming from regional circulation-driven cooling.
suggested_ui: Looping conveyor-belt ocean arrow; downstream dominoes: Europe Climate, Monsoons, Fisheries, Sea Level.

name: Labrador-Irminger Sea convection collapse
near_term_status: Possible at current warming; could occur over about a decade in some assessments
warming_context: Identified as possible at current warming and can become likely through the Paris-range warming band.
primary_causes: Freshwater input; ocean warming; salinity/density changes; weakened deep convection.
effects: Regional North Atlantic cooling; changed weather patterns in Europe and North America; monsoon shifts.
interactions: Related to broader North Atlantic circulation; can interact with AMOC dynamics and Greenland meltwater.
domino_summary: Freshening/warming -> weaker deep convection -> subpolar circulation change -> weather and monsoon disruptions.
app_card_summary: A faster circulation domino for showing that not all climate tipping changes unfold slowly.
misinformation_angle: Common misunderstanding: cold spells or regional cooling disprove warming. Prompt: identify whether the claim confuses local weather/circulation with global climate.
suggested_ui: Small swirling current symbol; downstream dominoes: Weather Patterns, North Atlantic, Monsoons.

name: Southern Ocean overturning slowdown/collapse
near_term_status: Uncertain but recognized as an ocean-atmosphere circulation tipping candidate
warming_context: Global Tipping Points Report identifies Southern Ocean overturning circulation as one of four ocean/atmosphere circulation tipping points.
primary_causes: Antarctic meltwater; warming; salinity and density changes; wind/circulation shifts.
effects: Changed ocean heat/carbon uptake; oxygen/nutrient distribution shifts; Antarctic ecosystem stress; feedbacks to ice-sheet stability.
interactions: Can influence Antarctic ice shelves, carbon uptake, marine ecosystems, and global heat redistribution.
domino_summary: Meltwater/warming -> circulation slowdown -> altered heat and carbon uptake -> Antarctic/ocean ecosystem impacts.
app_card_summary: A background-system domino: less visible to users but important for global heat, carbon, and marine ecosystems.
misinformation_angle: Common gap: invisible ocean systems are ignored because impacts are not obvious. Prompt: ask what measurements would reveal hidden system changes.
suggested_ui: World-ocean circulation ring; downstream dominoes: Carbon Uptake, Antarctic Ice, Marine Ecosystems.

name: Barents Sea ice abrupt loss
near_term_status: Possible around 1.5°C and can become likely through the Paris-range warming band
warming_context: Identified in the 2022 reassessment as becoming possible around 1.5°C and likely further through the Paris range.
primary_causes: Ocean warming; Arctic amplification; sea-ice albedo loss; atmospheric circulation shifts.
effects: Regional ecosystem disruption; altered weather patterns; increased ocean heat absorption; impacts to Arctic species and communities.
interactions: Albedo feedback contributes to regional warming; interacts with Arctic ecosystems and potentially atmospheric circulation.
domino_summary: Warming -> sea ice loss -> darker ocean absorbs heat -> more regional warming -> ecosystem/weather impacts.
app_card_summary: A compact visual domino for explaining albedo feedback without making the whole app map-based.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: sea ice comes and goes naturally. Prompt: compare seasonal variation with long-term trend and feedback mechanism.
suggested_ui: Sea-ice tile flipping from white to dark blue; downstream dominoes: Albedo, Arctic Heat, Marine Habitat.

name: Mountain glacier loss
near_term_status: Possible around 1.5°C and likely in the 2-3°C/current-policy range in reassessments
warming_context: Committed mountain glacier loss becomes likely in 2-3°C scenarios according to the 2022 reassessment explainer.
primary_causes: Rising air temperature; reduced snowpack; altered precipitation; black carbon deposition.
effects: Reduced seasonal water storage; water-supply stress for downstream communities; hydropower/agriculture impacts; flood hazards from glacial lakes.
interactions: Water insecurity cascades into food, energy, migration, and economic stress; glacier loss compounds heat/drought impacts.
domino_summary: Warming -> glacier retreat -> less dry-season meltwater -> water stress -> food/energy/community impacts.
app_card_summary: A human-impact domino: melting mountains become a water-security problem downstream.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: glacier retreat is only local scenery loss. Prompt: trace who depends on seasonal meltwater.
suggested_ui: Mountain with shrinking ice cap; downstream dominoes: Water Supply, Agriculture, Hydropower, Flood Risk.

name: West African monsoon shift
near_term_status: Recognized as a circulation tipping point; threshold and direction are uncertain
warming_context: Global Tipping Points Report identifies West African monsoon as one of four ocean/atmosphere circulation tipping points.
primary_causes: Greenhouse warming; aerosol forcing; land-atmosphere feedbacks; ocean temperature gradients.
effects: Large shifts in rainfall; agricultural impacts; water-security stress; possible greening or drying depending on system response.
interactions: Rainfall changes affect food security, livelihoods, migration pressure, and ecosystem states; can interact with Sahel vegetation dynamics.
domino_summary: Climate forcing -> monsoon shift -> rainfall redistribution -> agriculture/water impacts -> livelihood and migration stress.
app_card_summary: A social-ecological domino where a circulation shift can become a food and livelihood issue.
misinformation_angle: Common oversimplification: more rain is automatically good. Prompt: ask where, when, and how fast rainfall changes occur.
suggested_ui: Cloud/rain icon over Sahel; downstream dominoes: Food Security, Water, Livelihoods, Migration.

name: Sahel greening
near_term_status: Could become likely in the 2-3°C/current-policy range; impacts are mixed and regionally disruptive
warming_context: The 2022 reassessment explainer notes Sahel greening may become likely in 2-3°C scenarios and could disrupt livelihoods even if it seems positive.
primary_causes: Monsoon/rainfall shifts; vegetation-rainfall feedbacks; land-use changes; warming-driven circulation changes.
effects: Changed vegetation cover; livelihood disruption; altered rainfall patterns for neighboring regions; ecosystem transition.
interactions: Connected to West African monsoon dynamics; can influence agriculture, grazing, migration, and regional water availability.
domino_summary: Rainfall shift -> vegetation expansion -> land-atmosphere feedback -> livelihood/ecosystem changes.
app_card_summary: A nuance card: a 'green' tipping point can still create winners, losers, and regional instability.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading framing: any greening proves climate change is beneficial. Prompt: evaluate distributional impacts and what neighboring regions lose.
suggested_ui: Dryland-to-grass gradient; downstream dominoes: Livelihoods, Rainfall Redistribution, Land Use.

name: Lake eutrophication
near_term_status: Can occur locally/regional under nutrient pollution and warming; useful for teaching fast ecosystem tipping
warming_context: Global Tipping Points Report identifies lake eutrophication among biosphere tipping points; cascade literature often uses linked lake chains as examples.
primary_causes: Nutrient pollution; agricultural runoff; warming; stratification; low oxygen conditions.
effects: Algal blooms; fish kills; drinking-water problems; biodiversity loss; recreation/economic losses.
interactions: Upstream nutrient loading can cascade through connected lakes; warming worsens oxygen depletion and bloom risk.
domino_summary: Nutrient runoff + heat -> algal bloom -> oxygen loss -> fish kill -> water-quality and biodiversity collapse.
app_card_summary: A clear, local domino that makes tipping points feel understandable and less abstract.
misinformation_angle: Common misunderstanding: pollution and climate are separate issues. Prompt: identify co-drivers that combine to lower a tipping threshold.
suggested_ui: Lake icon turning green; downstream dominoes: Water Quality, Fish, Public Health, Recreation.

name: Fisheries collapse
near_term_status: Context-dependent; driven by exploitation plus ecosystem/climate stress
warming_context: Global Tipping Points Report identifies fishery collapse among biosphere tipping points.
primary_causes: Overfishing; ocean warming; habitat loss; acidification; food-web shifts; management failure.
effects: Food insecurity; livelihood loss; ecosystem imbalance; economic disruption in coastal communities.
interactions: Coral reef loss, ocean heat, and acidification can reduce habitat/productivity; social stress can increase overexploitation pressure.
domino_summary: Overfishing + warming -> stock decline -> ecosystem imbalance -> livelihood loss -> pressure for further extraction.
app_card_summary: A bridge between environmental and human systems: ecological collapse quickly becomes a community and food-security issue.
misinformation_angle: Common misleading claim: fish populations simply move, so no harm occurs. Prompt: distinguish relocation from collapse, access loss, and food-web disruption.
suggested_ui: Fish-school icon thinning out; downstream dominoes: Food Security, Livelihoods, Ecosystem Balance.

name: Mangrove and seagrass meadow die-off
near_term_status: Vulnerable around/above 1.5°C according to Global Tipping Points summary; local pressures can lower thresholds
warming_context: Global Tipping Points Report notes mangroves and seagrass meadows start to become vulnerable after passing 1.5°C.
primary_causes: Marine heat; sea-level rise; coastal development; pollution; sediment changes; water-quality decline.
effects: Loss of nursery habitat; reduced blue carbon storage; weaker coastal protection; biodiversity and fishery impacts.
interactions: Reef/fishery decline can compound coastal livelihood risks; sea-level rise and development squeeze coastal habitats.
domino_summary: Heat/pollution/sea-level pressure -> coastal habitat die-off -> less nursery habitat and carbon storage -> fishery/coastal-risk impacts.
app_card_summary: A coastal resilience domino: losing living shorelines removes both habitat and protection.
misinformation_angle: Common blind spot: coastal plants are treated as scenery, not infrastructure. Prompt: identify ecosystem services being lost.
suggested_ui: Seagrass/mangrove icon with shoreline shield; downstream dominoes: Blue Carbon, Fisheries, Coastal Protection.
